steps.title: Identify the blank’s role
steps.content:
The sentence says some colors seem to be more ______ viewers than others, and the example confirms that people find blues and greens more appealing. The blank must describe a positive effect colors have on viewers’ preferences — something that makes a color more desirable or pleasing to them.
  • Choice A — ‘confusing for’: Implies colors create confusion in viewers — opposite of the “appealing” relationship the example describes. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘attractive to’: Directly captures the idea that some colors draw viewers in more than others, aligning with the “more appealing” example. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘corrected by’: Implies viewers fix or adjust colors — makes no logical sense in context. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘similar to’: Implies colors resemble viewers in some way — incoherent and contradicts the contrast the example establishes. ✗

steps.title: Select the matching choice
steps.content: Colors that are more “attractive to” viewers naturally prompt viewers to find them more appealing — the phrase fits the blank precisely. That confirms B is correct.
